volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 5.2.672 - Section 5.3.723)

sections.label: Section 5.2.672
sections.original: Quid cessas , miles , hanc huic uxorem dare ?
sections.translation: Why do you hesitate , Captain , to give her as a wife to him ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.673
sections.translation: If she is agreable .

sections.label: Section 5.2.673b
sections.translation: My brother , it is my wish .

sections.label: Section 5.2.673c
sections.translation: So be it then .

sections.label: Section 5.2.673d
sections.translation: You do rightly .

sections.label: Section 5.2.674
sections.translation: Captain , do you betroth me to her for my wife ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.674b
sections.translation: I do betroth her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.675
sections.original: Et ego hoc idem una spondeo .
sections.translation: And to him do I promise support at my expense .

sections.label: Section 5.2.675b
sections.translation: You do kindly . But ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.2.676
sections.original: sed eccúm lenonem , incedit , thensaurum meum .
sections.translation: ⋯ But see , here ’ s the Procurer coming ; my treasurer [*] , I mean .

sections.label: Section 5.3.679
sections.original: Argentariis male credi qui aiunt , nugas praedicant :
sections.translation: Those who say [*] it ’ s bad for bankers to be trusted , utter nonsense ;

sections.label: Section 5.3.680
sections.original: nam et bene et male credi dico ; id adeo ego hodie expertus sum .
sections.translation: I say it ’ s neither good nor bad for them to be trusted , and that , I ’ ve fully experienced this day . They are not [*] badly trusted , who never repay , but with whom it is lost outright . As for example , before this Lyco paid me the ten minae , he had to go to every banker ’ s counter . After there were no proceeds , I summoned the fellow with much noise ; he appealed against me to the court . I was most confoundedly afraid that this day he ’ d be settling accounts with me before the Praetor [*] ;

sections.label: Section 5.3.685
sections.original: verum amici compulerunt : reddit argentum domo .
sections.translation: but my friends forced him , so he paid the money at home . Now I ’ m resolved to make haste to my house .

sections.label: Section 5.3.686b
sections.translation: Hallo ! you Procurer ; I want you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.687
sections.translation: And I want you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.687b
sections.translation: But I don ’ t want either of you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.687c
sections.translation: Stay this instant , will you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.688
sections.translation: And make you haste to disgorge the money with all despatch .

sections.label: Section 5.3.688b
sections.translation: What have you to do with me ? ( To the CAPTAIN .) Or what have you ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.689b
sections.original: Quía ego ex te hódie faciam pilum catapultarium
sections.translation: Because this day I shall be making a javelin of you , discharged from a catapulta ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.690
sections.original: atque ita te nervo torquebo , ítidem ut catapultae solent .
sections.translation: and twist you with the string [*] , just as the catapultas are in the habit of doing .

sections.label: Section 5.3.691
sections.original: Delicatum te hodie faciam , cum catello ut accubes ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll this day make a coxcomb of you , to be sleeping with a puppy [*] in your bed — an iron one , I mean .

sections.label: Section 5.3.692b
sections.original: At ego vos ambo in robusto carcere
sections.translation: But I ’ ll make you both to be rotting in a strong-barred prison .

sections.label: Section 5.3.693b
sections.original: Collum obstringe , abduce istum in malam crucem .
sections.translation: Seize him by the throat , and away with him to extreme torture .

sections.label: Section 5.3.694
sections.translation: However that is , he ’ ll be going there of his own accord .

sections.label: Section 5.3.694b
sections.translation: O Gods and men , your aid !

sections.label: Section 5.3.695
sections.original: hocine pacto indemnatum atque intestatum me abripi ?
sections.translation: that I , uncondemned , and without evidence against me , should thus be dragged along ! Prithee , Planesium , and you , Phaedromus , do give me assistance .

sections.label: Section 5.3.697
sections.original: Frater , obsecro te , noli hunc condemnatum perdere .
sections.translation: Brother , I do beseech you , don ’ t ruin him uncondemned ; he treated me kindly and modestly at his house .

sections.label: Section 5.3.698b
sections.translation: That was through no inclination of his own ; give you thanks to this Aesculapius [*] ( pointing to the Temple ) that you ’ ve preserved your chastity ;

sections.label: Section 5.3.700
sections.original: nam si valuisset , iam pridem quoquo posset mitteret .
sections.translation: for if he had been well , he ’ d long ago have packed you off wherever he could .

sections.label: Section 5.3.701
sections.original: Animum advortite , ego si possum hoc inter vos componere .
sections.translation: Now attend to me , both of you , if I can arrange between you . ( To the CAPTAIN .) You let him go . Procurer , come you this way . I ’ ll pronounce my opinion , if indeed you are ready to abide by what I shall decide you should do .

sections.label: Section 5.3.703b
sections.translation: We leave it to you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.704
sections.original: Dum quidem hercle ita iudices , ne quisquam a me argentum auferat .
sections.translation: So long , i ’ faith , as you give a decision to the effect that no one is to take the money away from me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.705
sections.translation: What , not that which you promised ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.705b
sections.translation: I , promised ? How ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.705c
sections.translation: With your tongue .

sections.label: Section 5.3.705d
sections.translation: With that same tongue I now gainsay it ; that was given me by Nature for the purpose of speaking , not of losing my property .

sections.label: Section 5.3.707
sections.translation: He ’ s trifling ; seize the fellow by the throat .

sections.label: Section 5.3.707b
sections.translation: I ’ ll at once then do as you bid me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.708
sections.original: Quando vir bonus es , responde quod rogo .
sections.translation: Since you are an honest man , answer me this that I ask you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.708b
sections.translation: Ask me what you please .

sections.label: Section 5.3.709
sections.original: Promistin , si liberali quisquam hanc assereret manu ,
sections.translation: Did you not promise , that if any one should show that she ( pointing to PLANESIUM ) was freeborn ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.710
sections.translation: you would pay back all the money ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.710b
sections.translation: I don ’ t remember saying so .

sections.label: Section 5.3.711
sections.translation: What , do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.711b
sections.original: Nego hercle vero . quo praesente ? quo in loco ?
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really do deny it . In whose presence ? In what place was it ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.712
sections.original: Me ipso praesente et Lycone tarpezita .
sections.translation: In my own presence , and that of Lyco the banker .

sections.label: Section 5.3.712b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you hold your tongue ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.713
sections.translation: I shan ’ t hold my tongue .

sections.label: Section 5.3.713b
sections.original: Non ego te flocci facio ; ne me territes .
sections.translation: I don ’ t care a rush for you ; don ’ t be bullying me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.714
sections.original: Me ipso praesente et Lycone factum est .
sections.translation: In my own presence and that of Lyco it took place .

sections.label: Section 5.3.714b
sections.translation: I quite believe you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.715
sections.original: nunc adeo , ut tu scire possis , leno , meam sententiam :
sections.translation: Now , therefore , Procurer , that you may know my judgment , ( pointing to PLANESIUM ) she is a free woman ; he ( pointing to the CAPTAIN ) is her brother , and she is his sister ; she is engaged to me ; do you restore him the money ; that ’ s my decision .

sections.label: Section 5.3.718
sections.original: Tu autem in nervo iam iacebis , nisi mi argentum redditur .
sections.translation: And as for you , you shall be clapt in prison at once , unless the money ’ s returned me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.719
sections.original: Hercle istam rem iudicasti perfidiose , Phaedrome .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , Phaedromus , you have given this decision corruptly .

sections.label: Section 5.3.720
sections.original: et tibi oberit et te , miles , di deaeque perduint .
sections.translation: It shall both be bad for you , and you , Captain ,— may the Gods and Goddesses confound you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.721
sections.translation: Then follow me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.721b
sections.translation: Follow you where ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.721c
sections.translation: To my banker — to the Praetor [*] ; for it ’ s there that I pay my money to all persons to whom I ’ m indebted .

sections.label: Section 5.3.723
sections.original: Ego te in nervom , haud ad praetorem hinc rapiam , ni argentum refers .
sections.translation: I ’ ll be carrying you hence to prison , and not to the Praetor , if you don ’ t pay back the money .
